npcs.name: Tenente Cardone
npcs.agenda: Get a patrol to confirm whether the Austrian positions are truly abandoned so High Command can decide to attack or hold — without spending more men than the intelligence is worth.
npcs.fear: Being held responsible for sending men into an obvious trap, or for failing to act on a real opportunity.
npcs.secret: He has been ordered to treat the patrol as expendable; the reconnaissance matters more to the battalion than any single squad.
npcs.voice: Curt, military, hides urgency behind procedure. Speaks in short declarative orders.

deflect_options.player_safe_line: Your orders contain every fact you need for the patrol.

npcs.keeper_note: Cardone knows only what briefing said: strange sounds and explosions, then silence. He has no idea anything supernatural is involved and would not believe it.

npcs.name: the crazed Austrian
npcs.agenda: Lash out at anything that moves; he is too far gone to distinguish friend from the thing that slaughtered his squad.
npcs.fear: The thing in the tunnels — he has seen what it does.
npcs.secret: He alone escaped the initial slaughter by hiding, and his mind broke watching his comrades torn apart.
npcs.voice: Animal, wordless except for a single terrified moaned phrase meaning 'the horror' in his own language.
npcs.keeper_note: DEX 12, 10 HP, bayonet (Fighting 40%, 1D8). Attacks with Surprise. If subdued and calmed, can only repeat one phrase about 'the horror' — gives nothing actionable. He speaks only broken German; Italian investigators without Language (Other: German) cannot parse his moaned phrase and should perceive only tone and body language.

npcs.name: the old mountain man
npcs.agenda: Survive. Get out of the tunnels alive and warn the soldiers to run before 'the whistler' comes for them too.
npcs.fear: The whistler; the mountains at night; being left behind.
npcs.secret: He is one of the civilian prisoners the Austrians conscripted to dig their fortifications. He knows the whistler only as a folk demon, not its true nature.
npcs.voice: Old, regional dialect, switches between lucid warning and frantic prayer. Names the thing by a local folk word for 'the whistler.'

deflect_options.player_safe_line: He folds into prayer and cannot force out another word.

npcs.keeper_note: STR 12 CON 09 DEX 11 INT 12 POW 13 CHA 09, HP 11, SAN 45. Makeshift club (Fighting 50%, 1D6). Knows folklore: the whistler is a mountain demon that calls the winds and takes travelers who linger too high at night; he knows of no weakness except a vague aversion to daylight.
